fa: 10,000 Athenians defeated 25,000 Persians; a runner allegedly ran 40km to Athens to announce victory — inspiring the modern marathon
King Leonidas and 300 Spartans held off the Persian army for 3 days, becoming one of history's most celebrated last stands
The Greek fleet destroyed the Persian navy, ending the Persian invasion and saving Western civilization according to many historians
Alexander the Great defeated Darius III despite being outnumbered 5 to 1, effectively ending the Achaemenid Persian Empire
Hannibal's double envelopment destroyed 70,000 Romans — still studied as the most perfect tactical encirclement in military history
Scipio Africanus defeated Hannibal, ending the Second Punic War and establishing Rome as the dominant Mediterranean power
Caesar besieged 80,000 Gauls while simultaneously holding off a 250,000-strong relief army — ending Gallic independence
This naval battle ended the Roman Republic and established the Roman Empire under Augustus Caesar
Three Roman legions (20,000 men) were completely annihilated, halting Roman expansion into Germania permanently
Emperor Valens was killed and the Roman army destroyed — marking the beginning of the fall of the Western Roman Empire
An army of 80,000 defeated a force of 800,000 — the decisive battle that preserved Chinese culture south of the Yangtze
Charles Martel stopped the Muslim advance into Western Europe, a pivotal moment in the shaping of Christian Europe
William the Conqueror defeated King Harold II — transforming English language, culture and law through Norman influence
The crushing Byzantine defeat opened Anatolia to Turkish settlement, eventually leading to the fall of the Byzantine Empire
Saladin's decisive victory led to the recapture of Jerusalem and effectively ended the First Kingdom of Jerusalem
English longbowmen destroyed the French cavalry charge — the first major battle of the Hundred Years War
Henry V's longbowmen devastated the French cavalry — immortalized by Shakespeare in Henry V
The 1,000-year Byzantine Empire fell to Mehmed II; the fall ended the Middle Ages and triggered the Renaissance
Richard III was killed — the last English king to die in battle — ending the Wars of the Roses and starting the Tudor dynasty
The only clash between Tang China and the Islamic Caliphate; Arab victory spread Islam into Central Asia and ended Chinese westward expansion
Prince Lazar was killed; Serbia fell under Ottoman rule for nearly 500 years — Kosovo remains a symbol of Serbian national identity
Timur defeated and captured Sultan Bayezid I — the only Ottoman sultan ever taken prisoner, nearly collapsing the empire
The first time the Mongol advance was permanently halted — a pivotal moment that saved Islam and stopped Mongol western expansion
Hulagu Khan destroyed the Islamic Golden Age's capital, massacring up to 800,000 people and destroying the Grand Library of Baghdad
The last Song loyalists fought their final battle; the prime minister jumped into the sea with the child emperor, ending Chinese resistance

be: Grand Duchy of Moscow vs Golden Horde
fa: Dmitry Donskoy's first victory over the Mongols; sparked Russian national identity and began the long decline of Mongol power in Russia
The Mongols annihilated a combined Polish-German army; only Ögedei Khan's death prevented a full-scale invasion of Western Europe

be: Mongol Empire vs Kingdom of Hungary
fa: Batu Khan destroyed the Hungarian army, killing up to half the country's population — Hungary never fully recovered
Genghis Khan cornered Jalal ad-Din on the Indus river banks, ending the Khwarazmian Empire — the path to Persia lay open
The final naval battle of the Genpei War; the child emperor Antoku drowned as the Taira clan was destroyed, ending samurai civil war
Nobunaga's volley fire with 3,000 arquebusiers annihilated Takeda's legendary cavalry — revolutionizing Japanese warfare
The decisive battle that unified Japan; the Tokugawa shogunate ruled for 265 years of relative peace until 1868
Saigo Takamori's last stand with 500 samurai against 30,000 soldiers — the final battle of the samurai era in Japan
The most famous clash of Japan's two greatest rivals; legend says Kenshin personally attacked Shingen during the chaos
Babur's use of artillery and firearms defeated a larger force, founding the Mughal Empire that would rule India for 300 years
Akbar's regent Bairam Khan defeated Hindu king Hemu despite numerical inferiority, saving the Mughal Empire from collapse
The Marathas' greatest defeat — 40,000-100,000 killed including their leadership, ending their pan-Indian ambitions permanently

be: British East India Company vs Maratha Confederacy
fa: Wellington called it 'the bloodiest battle I ever saw'; despite huge losses, Britain broke Maratha power in western India
Simón Bolívar liberated New Granada (Colombia) in a decisive 2-hour battle — the turning point of South American independence

be: San Martín's forces vs Spanish royalists
fa: San Martín's crossing of the Andes led to this surprise victory — liberating Chile from Spanish rule within days
The final battle of South American independence; Spain surrendered its last mainland South American territory
Largest land battle in South American history; Paraguay lost 60% of its population in the war — one of history's most devastating conflicts
Moroccan muskets destroyed the Songhai cavalry — ending the last great West African empire and reshaping trans-Saharan trade routes
20,000 Zulu warriors armed mainly with spears defeated a British force equipped with rifles — the worst British defeat in colonial Africa
Ethiopia became the only African nation to defeat a European colonial power — preserving its independence and inspiring pan-African movements
Kitchener's forces killed 11,000 Mahdists in 5 hours; Winston Churchill charged with the 21st Lancers in the last British cavalry charge

be: Ottoman Empire vs Kingdom of Hungary
fa: Suleiman the Magnificent destroyed the Hungarian army in 2 hours; King Louis II drowned fleeing — opening Central Europe to Ottoman rule
Selim I's artillery crushed the Safavid cavalry; the battle fixed the Sunni-Shia border that still roughly defines Iran-Turkey today

be: Knights of Malta vs Ottoman Empire
fa: 5,000 Knights of Malta and Maltese held off 40,000 Ottomans for 4 months — halting Ottoman western Mediterranean expansion
The largest naval battle of the 16th century halted Ottoman naval expansion in the Mediterranean; Cervantes fought and lost his hand here
The Armada's destruction established English naval supremacy and helped Protestantism survive in northern Europe

be: Holy Roman Empire & Poland vs Ottoman Empire
fa: The last great Ottoman advance into Europe was repelled — beginning the long Ottoman decline; croissants were allegedly invented to celebrate
Marlborough and Eugene of Savoy's masterpiece; ended French dominance of Europe for the first time in generations
Peter the Great's decisive victory over Charles XII ended Swedish dominance in Northern Europe and confirmed Russia as a major power

be: British East India Company vs Nawab of Bengal
fa: Robert Clive's victory through bribery laid the foundation for British colonial rule over India
The pivotal American victory convinced France to enter the Revolutionary War on the American side — turning the tide of the war
Nelson's fleet destroyed the Franco-Spanish navy but Nelson was killed; Britain gained unchallenged naval supremacy for a century
Napoleon's greatest tactical victory — lured the Allies into a trap by deliberately weakening his right flank
The bloodiest day of the Napoleonic Wars — 70,000 casualties; Russia's refusal to surrender despite the loss doomed Napoleon
Napoleon's final defeat ended his rule and the Napoleonic Wars — 'Waterloo' became synonymous with final, crushing defeat
Sam Houston's 18-minute battle defeated Santa Anna and secured Texas independence — fought to cries of 'Remember the Alamo!'
The disastrous Charge of the Light Brigade — 600 cavalrymen charged into Russian artillery due to a misunderstood order
The bloodiest battle of the Civil War (50,000 casualties) — Pickett's Charge failed catastrophically; the Confederacy never recovered
Napoleon III was captured — ending the Second Empire; Prussia proclaimed the German Empire in Versailles' Hall of Mirrors
General Custer's entire command was annihilated — 'Custer's Last Stand' became America's most famous military disaster
20,000 Zulu warriors armed mainly with spears defeated a British force equipped with rifles — the worst British defeat in colonial Africa
Japan destroyed the entire Russian Baltic Fleet in 45 minutes — the first time an Asian power defeated a European great power in modern times
'The Miracle of the Marne' — taxis of Paris rushed 6,000 soldiers to halt the German advance, saving Paris and forcing trench warfare
The disastrous amphibious assault cost 130,000 lives; its failure shaped Australian and New Zealand national identity (ANZAC Day)
The longest battle of WWI (10 months, 700,000 casualties) — 'The Meatgrinder' became the symbol of the war's futility
60,000 British casualties on the first day alone — the bloodiest day in British military history
The largest naval battle of WWI; though Germany inflicted more damage, Britain retained control of the North Sea
300,000 casualties fighting through mud so thick soldiers drowned — soldiers called it 'The Battle of the Mud'
The largest battle in American history — 1.2 million US troops; Sergeant Alvin York single-handedly captured 132 Germans
The RAF's victory over the Luftwaffe prevented a German invasion; Churchill said 'Never was so much owed by so many to so few'
The US sank 4 Japanese carriers while losing 1 — the pivotal Pacific battle that permanently shifted naval power to the United States
Montgomery's defeat of Rommel's Africa Korps; Churchill said 'Before Alamein we never had a victory; after Alamein we never had a defeat'
The largest and bloodiest battle in history (2 million casualties); Field Marshal Paulus surrendered — the turning point of WWII
The largest tank battle in history; after Kursk, Germany was never able to launch a major offensive on the Eastern Front again
The largest amphibious operation in history — 156,000 troops landed on 5 beaches; opened the Western Front that defeated Nazi Germany
Germany's last major offensive — 'Nuts!' replied General McAuliffe to a German surrender demand; 76,000 American casualties
The iconic flag-raising photo was taken here; 26,000 US and 22,000 Japanese casualties — one of the bloodiest Pacific battles
The final battle of WWII in Europe — 360,000 Soviet and 100,000 German casualties; Hitler committed suicide as Soviets entered the city
The bloodiest Pacific battle — 12,000 Americans, 110,000 Japanese and 100,000 Okinawan civilians died; prompted use of atomic bombs
MacArthur's surprise amphibious landing behind enemy lines reversed the Korean War — considered one of history's greatest military gambles
The decisive battle ended French colonialism in Indochina — the only time a guerrilla army defeated a Western colonial power in pitched battle
Israel defeated three Arab armies in 6 days, tripling its territory — one of the most rapid military victories of the 20th century

be: USA & South Vietnam vs North Vietnam
fa: The deadliest battle of the Vietnam War for the US Marines; the Tet Offensive shocked American public opinion and turned the tide against the war
'Black Hawk Down' — US forces lost 18 soldiers and 2 helicopters; the battle led the US to withdraw from Somalia
The largest urban combat operation since Hue in 1968; 95 US deaths, 800+ insurgents killed in door-to-door fighting
